identificationModule.briefTitle: Trial to Evaluate Cardiovascular and Other Long-term Outcomes With Semaglutide in Subjects With Type 2 Diabetes
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Long-term, Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Multinational, Multi-centre Trial to Evaluate Cardiovascular and Other Long-term Outcomes With Semaglutide in Subjects With Type 2 Diabetes (SUSTAIN™ 6 - Long-term Outcomes)

outcomeMeasures.title: Time From Randomisation to First Occurrence of a MACE, Defined as Cardiovascular Death, Non-fatal Myocardial Infarction, or Non-fatal Stroke
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentage of subjects experiencing a first event of a major adverse cardiovascular event (MACE), defined as cardiovascular (CV) death, non-fatal myocardial infarction (MI), or non-fatal stroke.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all the randomised subjects. As specified in the protocol, the two semaglutide dose arms (semaglutide 0.5 mg + semaglutide 1.0 mg) and the two placebo dose arms (placebo 0.5 mg + placebo 1.0 mg) were pooled for the analysis of this endpoint.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Time from randomisation up to end of follow-up (scheduled at week 109)

groups.title: Semaglutide
groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of semaglutide 0.5 mg or 1.0 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Injections were administered subcutaneously in the thigh, abdomen or upper arm, at any time of day irrespective of meals. The injections were to be administered on the same weekday during the trial. Semaglutide was initiated at a dose of 0.25 mg and was escalated (doubled) to 0.5 mg after 4 weeks. Hence the target dose of 0.5 mg was achieved after 4 weeks of treatment and the target dose of 1.0 mg was achieved after 8 weeks of treatment. Each subject was treated for 104 weeks.

groups.title: Placebo
groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of placebo 0.5 mg or 1.0 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Injections were administered subcutaneously in the thigh, abdomen or upper arm, at any time of day irrespective of meals. The injections were to be administered on the same weekday during the trial. Placebo was initiated at a dose of 0.25 mg and was escalated (doubled) to 0.5 mg after 4 weeks. Hence the target dose of 0.5 mg was achieved after 4 weeks of treatment and the target dose of 1.0 mg was achieved after 8 weeks of treatment. Each subject was treated for 104 weeks.

analyses.groupDescription: The primary endpoint was analysed using a stratified Cox proportional hazards model with treatment group (semaglutide, placebo) as fixed factor. Assuming the same population MACE risk for the semaglutide and placebo groups (i.e., the population hazards ratio \[HR\] equals 1), a total minimum of 122 events were needed in order to have at least 90% power to ascertain that the upper two-sided 95% confidence limit for the HR was less than 1.8.
analyses.nonInferiorityComment: Non-inferiority of semaglutide versus placebo was considered to be confirmed if the upper limit of the two-sided 95% CI for the HR was below 1.8 or equivalent if the p-value for the one-sided test of: H0: HR ≥ 1.8 against Ha: HR \<1.8 was less than 2.5% (or equivalent to 5% for a two-sided test).
analyses.pValueComment: The 'p-value' is for the two-sided Wald test of non-inferiority with limit 1.8.

analyses.groupDescription: A post hoc analysis of superiority of semaglutide versus placebo was performed based on the pre-specified Cox proportional hazard analysis using the two-sided Wald test of no difference, with treatment (semaglutide, placebo) as fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).
analyses.pValueComment: The 'p-value' is for the two-sided Wald test of no difference.

outcomeMeasures.title: Time From Randomisation to First Occurrence of an Expanded Composite Cardiovascular Outcome
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentage of subjects experiencing first occurrence of an expanded composite CV outcome (defined as either MACE, revascularisation \[coronary and peripheral\], unstable angina requiring hospitalisation or hospitalisation for heart failure)
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all the randomised subjects. As specified in the protocol, the two semaglutide dose arms (semaglutide 0.5 mg + semaglutide 1.0 mg) and the two placebo dose arms (placebo 0.5 mg + placebo 1.0 mg) were pooled for the analysis of this endpoint.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Time from randomisation up to end of follow-up (scheduled at week 109)

analyses.groupDescription: Analysis was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

outcomeMeasures.title: Time From Randomisation to Each Individual Component of the Expanded Composite Cardiovascular Outcome
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentage of subjects experiencing an event onset for each individual component of the expanded composite cardiovascular outcomes (defined as either MACE, revascularisation \[coronary and peripheral\], unstable angina requiring hospitalisation or hospitalisation for heart failure).
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all the randomised subjects. As specified in the protocol, the two semaglutide dose arms (semaglutide 0.5 mg + semaglutide 1.0 mg) and the two placebo dose arms (placebo 0.5 mg + placebo 1.0 mg) were pooled for the analysis of this endpoint.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Time from randomisation up to end of follow-up (scheduled at week 109)

groups.title: Semaglutide
groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of semaglutide 0.5 mg or 1.0 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Injections were administered subcutaneously in the thigh, abdomen or upper arm, at any time of day irrespective of meals. The injections were to be administered on the same weekday during the trial. analyses.groupDescription: Analysis for CV death was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

Analysis for non-fatal myocardial infarction was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

Analysis for non-fatal stroke was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

Analysis for revascularisation was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

Analysis for 'unstable angina requiring hospitalisation' was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

Analysis for hospitalisation for heart failure was done by Cox proportional hazards model with treatment (semaglutide; placebo) as a fixed factor and stratified by all possible combinations of the three stratification factors used in the randomisation procedure (in total 9 levels).

analyses.groupDescription: Analysis for total cholesterol was done using a mixed model for repeated measurements with treatment (4 levels) and stratification (9 levels) as fixed factors and baseline value as covariate, all nested within visit. The response and its baseline value were log-transformed before analysis.

outcomeMeasures.title: Incidence During the Trial in Other Treatment Outcomes: Hypoglycaemic Events
outcomeMeasures.description: Rates (event rate per 100 exposure years) of severe or blood glucose confirmed symptomatic hypoglycaemia defned as an episode that was severe according to the American diabetic association (ADA) classification or blood glucose (BG) confirmed by a PG value \<3.1 mmol/L (56 mg/dL) with symptoms consistent with hypoglycaemia.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all randomised subjects.

groups.title: Semaglutide 1.0 mg
groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of semaglutide 1.0 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Injections were administered subcutaneously in the thigh, abdomen or upper arm, at any time of day irrespective of meals. The injections were to be administered on the same weekday during the trial. Semaglutide was initiated at a dose of 0.25 mg and was escalated (doubled) to 0.5 mg after 4 weeks. Hence the target dose of 1.0 mg was achieved after 8 weeks of treatment. Each subject was treated for 104 weeks.

groups.title: Placebo 0.5 mg
groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of placebo 0.5 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Injections were administered subcutaneously in the thigh, abdomen or upper arm, at any time of day irrespective of meals. The injections were to be administered on the same weekday during the trial. Placebo was initiated at a dose of 0.25 mg and was escalated (doubled) to 0.5 mg after 4 weeks. Hence the target dose of 0.5 mg was achieved after 4 weeks of treatment. Each subject was treated for 104 weeks.

groups.description: Subjects received once-weekly dose of placebo 1.0 mg as an add-on to their standard-of-care treatment. Placebo was initiated at a dose of 0.25 mg and was escalated (doubled) to 0.5 mg after 4 weeks. Hence the target dose of 1.0 mg was achieved after 8 weeks of treatment. Each subject was treated for 104 weeks.

outcomeMeasures.title: Incidence During the Trial in Other Treatment Outcomes: Adverse Events
outcomeMeasures.description: Rates (event rate per 100 years of exposure) of treatment emergent adverse events.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all randomised subjects.
outcomeMeasures.unitOfMeasure: Event rate per 100 years of exposure

outcomeMeasures.title: Occurrence During the Trial in Other Treatment Outcomes: Anti-semaglutide Antibodies
outcomeMeasures.description: The percentage of subjects that tested positive for anti-semaglutide antibodies at any time point post-baseline during the trial, from week 0 to week 109.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all randomised subjects

outcomeMeasures.title: Change From Baseline to Last Assessment in the Trial in Other Treatment Outcomes: Patient Reported Outcome (PRO)
outcomeMeasures.description: Estimated mean change from baseline to last assessment in the trial in patient reported outcomes (PRO). PRO questionnaire (SF-36v2TM) measured the individual overall health related quality of life namely bodily pain, general health, mental component summary, mental health, physical component summary, physical functioning, role-emotional, role-physical, social functioning and vitality. The PRO scores were transformed to a 0-100 scale with higher scores indicating greater health related quality of life.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Full analysis set included all randomised subjects.

analyses.groupDescription: Analysis for bodily pain was done using a mixed model for repeated measurements with treatment (4 levels) and stratification (9 levels) as fixed factors and baseline value as covariate, all nested within visit.
